item.title: Fed’s Warsh Signals Revamp, But Offers Little Substance on Rates
item.description: Kevin Warsh began his tenure as chairman of the Federal Reserve with a solemn vow to curb inflation and a clear sign that he plans to swiftly revamp how the US central bank does its job.

item.title: Multinationals Shield Ireland From Iran Shock, Central Bank Says
item.description: Multinational companies are buoying Irish economic growth even as conflict in the Middle East dampens consumer demand, according to the Central Bank of Ireland.

item.title: Indonesia, Philippines Set to Hike Rates as Caution Prevails
item.description: Two of Asia’s beleaguered central banks are poised to tighten monetary policy further, staying on guard in case inflation and currency risks persist despite a potential deescalation of the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Taiwan Seen Holding Benchmark Rate Even as Inflation Speeds Up
item.description: Taiwan’s central bank is expected to leave its benchmark interest rate unchanged for a ninth straight quarter, though a roaring economy and accelerating inflation are creating pressure for it to join other Asian economies in turning hawkish.

item.title: Gold Holds Decline After Bets Increase on Fed Hike This Year
item.description: Gold held a loss after the US Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged and signaled a hike later in the year.

item.title: Fed’s Warsh Earns Points From Economists for ‘Slick’ Performance
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh’s highly anticipated maiden press conference on Wednesday was, according to many Fed watchers, smooth, polished and tightly controlled. Following the decision to hold rates steady, Warsh gave a statement and fielded questions from reporters.

item.title: New Zealand’s Economy Accelerated Before War Sapped Momentum
item.description: New Zealand’s economy sparked into life in the first three months of the year, responding to low interest rates and a lift in spending that largely pre-dated the Iran war.

item.title: BWX Agrees to License Nuclear Reactor Design After Activist Push
item.description: BWX Technologies Inc. has struck a deal to license its design for a small modular reactor, after activist investor Ananym Capital Management pushed the nuclear technology company to consider commercializing it.

item.title: Pimco Favors Australian Bonds, Betting on Rate Cuts Next Year
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co. is favoring five- to 10-year Australian debt, wagering the central bank will ultimately be forced to pivot to interest rate cuts to support a slowing economy.

item.title: Microsoft Makes Big AI Inroads in China by Selling OpenAI Models
item.description: Microsoft Corp. has built a big business selling AI models to Chinese companies despite the growing rivalry between the US and China over artificial intelligence.

item.title: The Fed Enters Its Warsh Era
item.description: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh wrapped his first policy meeting today, holding interest rates steady and debuting a shortened policy statement and one less dot on the dot plot.

item.title: Warsh Rocks Bond Market in Debut, Sparks Surge in Rate-Hike Bets
item.description: It didn’t take Kevin Warsh long to get the bond market’s attention.

item.title: Asian Stocks, Bonds to Fall on Hawkish Fed Signals: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks and bonds were poised to track US losses after the Federal Reserve signaled rates may need to rise further to contain inflation.

item.title: Crude Oil Edges Lower as Deal to Reopen Hormuz Goes Into Effect
item.description: Oil edged lower as traders assessed the interim US-Iran peace deal, with state television in the Islamic Republic and a US official saying that the pact had been signed digitally by the nations’ presidents.

item.title: Burnham Risks Looking More and More Like the Man He Would Replace
item.description: If Andy Burnham wins a parliamentary seat in the early hours of Friday, it is inevitable he’ll launch an assault on Keir Starmer’s job.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Cuts Interest Rate to 14.25% Even as Inflation Outlook Worsens
item.description: Brazil’s central bank cut its key interest rate by a quarter-point for a third straight meeting, cautiously pressing ahead with an unwinding of ultra-tight monetary policy despite a worsening inflation outlook.

item.title: Warsh Puts Stamp on Fed With Immediate Changes to Usual Routine
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh put his stamp on the US central bank Wednesday with a bevy of changes coming out of his first policy meeting, setting the stage for the wider overhaul of the institution he promised before he took the helm.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: FOMC利上げ巡り予想割れる、イラン合意への批判一蹴、小泉防衛相、レアアース巡り合意、依存ゼロに

item.title: What a Missing Dot Revealed at the Fed
item.description: For more than a decade, officials at the US Federal Reserve have shared their predictions on future interest rates via a small scatter chart published four times a year. Nineteen central bank policymakers can offer their projections, reflected anonymously on the dot plot.

item.title: BOJ Will Hike Rates Again by December, Says 90% of Economists
item.description: An overwhelming majority of Bank of Japan watchers anticipate the central bank will raise interest rates again by the end of this year, following a hike to the highest level since 1995 earlier this week, according to a Bloomberg survey.

item.title: Oaktree Private Credit Fund Redemptions Drop Below Key 5% Limit
item.description: An Oaktree Capital Management private credit fund saw redemption requests drop by nearly half in the second quarter, making it the first major firm to stem a growing exodus from the $1.8 trillion industry.

item.title: Apple Prepares Second-Generation iPhone Air for Spring 2027
item.description: Apple Inc. is preparing a second-generation iPhone Air for spring 2027, aiming to boost the appeal of the slimmed-down device, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Trump Says He’d Rather Not Have USMCA, Teasing Termination
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would rather the US not be a part of the US-Mexico-Canada trilateral trade pact, but sent mixed signals about an upcoming renewal deadline and stopped short of threatening outright to quit.

item.title: Dollar Rallies as Fed Officials Open Door to 2026 Rate Hike
item.description: The dollar surged the most in three months after Federal Reserve officials signaled growing support for interest-rate hikes this year.

item.title: Yen Slides to Weakest Level Versus Dollar Since July 2024
item.description: The yen slumped to its weakest level against the US dollar since July 2024, raising the risk of official intervention to prop it up.

item.title: Warsh Forms Task Force to Review Fed’s $6.7 Trillion Balance Sheet
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh said he is appointing a task force to examine the central bank’s $6.7 trillion balance sheet, a first step in addressing a policy issue he has long criticized.

item.title: UK Cabinet Pushes Starmer to Increase Defense Spending Amid Row
item.description: Some UK cabinet ministers are pushing Prime Minister Keir Starmer and his chancellor of the Exchequer, Rachel Reeves, to increase defense funding further, following John Healey’s resignation over the spending row.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Michele Says Fed Market Message Is to Brace for Hikes
item.description: JPMorgan Asset Management’s Bob Michele expressed surprise at the hawkish tilt from the Federal Open Market Committee under new Chairman Kevin Warsh, saying policymakers sense more risk from inflation pressures than Wall Street had expected.

item.title: Bass Pro Shops Is Buying a Luxury Florida Keys Fishing Lodge
item.description: Outdoor retailer Bass Pro Shops is buying a historic fishing lodge in the Florida Keys, adding another luxury hotel to its growing portfolio.

item.title: US Yields Jump as Fed Dot Plot Boosts Trader Bets on a 2026 Rate Hike
item.description: US Treasury yields leaped as Federal Reserve officials signaled they expect an interest-rate hike in the coming months, pushing traders to fully price in higher borrowing costs in 2026.
